title: Kaimoor Sanctuary
tagline: Kaimoor Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Uttar Pradesh

overview.short: Kaimoor Wildlife Sanctuary is located in Sonbhadra and Mirzapur districts of southeastern Uttar Pradesh.
overview.description: Kaimoor Wildlife Sanctuary is located in Sonbhadra and Mirzapur districts of southeastern Uttar Pradesh. The sanctuary reaches generally east and west along the Kaimur Range, extending to the Son River at its eastern end, and to the border of Madhya Pradesh at its western end. It was established in 1982. The vegetation consists of 500 square kilometers of verdant land.

topPlaces.name: Robertsganj
topPlaces.description: Robertsganj (Hindi: rŏbartsganj), is a city and a municipal board in Sonbhadra district in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. Robertsganj is located in the south-eastern corner of the state.

topPlaces.name: Churk
topPlaces.description: Churk Ghurma is a town and a nagar panchayat in Sonbhadra district in the state of Uttar Pradesh, India. The town is famous for a cement factory which was previously owned by state government.

topPlaces.name: Madhupur, Sonbhadra
topPlaces.description: Madhupur is a census town in Sonbhadra district in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. It is located 17 km from Robertsganj which is the administrative headquarters of Sonbhadra District.

topPlaces.name: Son View Point
topPlaces.description: Son View beautiful Point, also known as Eco Point Robertsganj, is an Eco Garden in Sonbhadra district, India. The panoramic view of Son Valley is best witnessed from this point.

items.note: Robertsganj (Hindi: rŏbartsganj), is a city and a municipal board in Sonbhadra district in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. Robertsganj is located in the south-eastern corner of the state.
Churk Ghurma is a town and a nagar panchayat in Sonbhadra district in the state of Uttar Pradesh, India. The town is famous for a cement factory which was previously owned by state government.
Madhupur is a census town in Sonbhadra district in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. It is located 17 km from Robertsganj which is the administrative headquarters of Sonbhadra District.
Son View beautiful Point, also known as Eco Point Robertsganj, is an Eco Garden in Sonbhadra district, India. The panoramic view of Son Valley is best witnessed from this point.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kaimoor Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kaimoor Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kaimoor Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Lal Bahadur Shastri Airport, Varanasi (88 km) and the nearest railway station is Varanasi Junction (72 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kaimoor Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kaimoor Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kaimoor Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Kaimoor Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kaimoor Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Robertsganj, Churk, Madhupur, Sonbhadra, Son View Point.
